instruction: Reply to Lena Ortiz about her scleral lens cleaning question. Tell her to rinse the lens with preservative-free saline before insertion, never use tap water, leave the hydrogen peroxide case closed for at least 6 hours overnight, and if the redness keeps happening after removal she should stop wearing the lens and book a visit.

rule_ref.rule_text: When he needs to communicate technical or patient-facing details, he prefers a draft to review first rather than having the message sent immediately.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: I like technical or patient-facing messages drafted first so I can review the wording before anything goes out.

attributes.subject: Question about scleral lens cleaning steps
attributes.body: Hi Eduardo, I'm still getting used to my scleral lenses and wanted to check the cleaning steps. Am I supposed to rinse them with saline or regular water before putting them in? Also, does the peroxide case need to sit all night, and what should I do if my eye still looks red after I take the lens out? Thanks, Lena

attributes.subject: August coating materials invoice
attributes.body: Your August invoice for anti-reflective coating materials is attached for processing.

attributes.subject: Lunch after Thursday training?
attributes.body: Checking whether you're free to grab lunch after the materials training on Thursday.
